title: 现在年轻人的婚恋观：为什么不想结婚？

youtube.publish_title: Why Young Chinese Are Not Getting Married | 现在年轻人的婚恋观

chapters.title: 进入话题：什么是“催婚”和“相亲”
婚姻观念的改变：不再“搭伙过日子”
父母的代沟与沟通方法

in_this_episode_you_will_learn: Common Chinese words for dating and marriage pressure (催婚, 相亲).
Why many young people in China are choosing to stay single.
How to express personal choices and boundaries in Chinese.
Cultural differences between generations regarding marriage.
description_intro: Are you feeling the pressure to get married? In this episode, Panpan and Laolu discuss a very hot topic in China: modern young people's views on marriage and relationships. You will learn useful expressions like 催婚 (push to marry) and 相亲 (blind dates).

We will also explore the reasons behind the low marriage rate among young people, such as high living pressure and the pursuit of personal freedom. This episode is perfect for HSK2-HSK3 learners to practice everyday conversational Chinese while understanding real modern culture.

blocks.purpose: 开场白，通过生活小故事自然引出被长辈询问结婚的困扰

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 大家好，欢迎来到我们的日常中文频道。我是盼盼。
segments.en: Hello everyone, welcome to our Daily Chinese Podcast. I'm Panpan.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 大家好，我是老路。
segments.en: Hi everyone, I'm Lao Lu.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 路哥，最近我真的有点怕接我妈的电话。每次电话一响，我就开始紧张。
segments.en: Lu-ge, lately I've honestly been a little scared to answer my mom's calls. Every time the phone rings, I start to tense up.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 怎么了？
segments.en: What happened?

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 因为每次一打电话，她就会问我一个特别让人头疼的问题。你猜猜是什么？
segments.en: Because every time she calls, she asks me one really headache-inducing question. Can you guess what it is?

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 我猜一下啊。是不是问你，什么时候带个男朋友回家？
segments.en: Let me guess. Is it, “When are you bringing a boyfriend home?”

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 对！完全正确。真的太准了。
segments.en: Yes! Exactly right. That was way too accurate.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 哈哈。
segments.en: Haha.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 不仅是我妈，上个周末我回老家，我的阿姨、叔叔，每一个看到我的亲戚都在问同样的问题。他们一直问，你年纪也不小了，什么时候结婚啊？有没有合适的人啊？真的让我觉得压力特别大。
segments.en: Not just my mom. I went back to my hometown last weekend, and every aunt, uncle, every relative who saw me asked the same thing. They kept asking, “You’re not that young anymore, so when are you getting married? Is there anyone suitable?” It really makes me feel a lot of pressure.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 哎呀，太可怕了。
segments.en: Oh no, that's terrifying.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 是啊。我觉得不仅是我，现在很多年轻人都有这个烦恼。所以今天，我们就来聊一聊这个话题：现在年轻人的婚恋观。
segments.en: Yeah. And I don't think it's just me. A lot of young people deal with this now. So today, let's talk about this topic: how young people today view love and marriage.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 好的，这个话题非常有意思。
segments.en: Sounds good. This is a very interesting topic.

blocks.purpose: 解释核心词汇“催婚”和“相亲”

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 刚才盼盼说的情况，在中文里有一个特别常用的词，叫做“催婚”。
segments.en: What Panpan just described has a very common term in Chinese: cuīhūn, being pressured to get married.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 催婚。
segments.en: Being pressured to marry.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 对。催，就是让你快一点做一件事情。比如，老板让你快点把工作做完，这叫催。父母或者长辈，一直告诉你快点结婚，让你觉得很着急，这就叫催婚。
segments.en: Right. 催 means urging someone to do something faster. For example, if your boss tells you to hurry up and finish your work, that's 催. When parents or elders keep telling you to get married soon and make you feel anxious, that's called 催婚.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 嗯，明白了。
segments.en: Mm, got it.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 为了让你早点结婚，父母经常会安排你去“相亲”。
segments.en: To get you married sooner, parents often arrange xiāngqīn, or blind dates.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 相亲？
segments.en: Blind date?

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 是的。相亲就是，父母或者朋友给你介绍一个不认识的人。你们两个人因为要结婚的目的，坐在一起喝咖啡、吃饭，互相了解。如果觉得合适，就继续谈恋爱。
segments.en: Yes. Xiāngqīn means your parents or friends introduce you to someone you don't know. The two of you meet for coffee or a meal with marriage in mind and get to know each other. If it feels like a good match, then you keep dating.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 天哪，相亲感觉太尴尬了。两个完全不认识的人坐在那里，像是在面试一样。要问对方的工作是什么，每个月赚多少钱，有没有买房子。这根本不浪漫啊。
segments.en: Oh wow, blind dates sound so awkward. Two complete strangers sitting there like it's a job interview. You ask what the other person does, how much they make a month, whether they've bought a home. There's nothing romantic about that.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 对。
segments.en: Exactly.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 可是父母觉得，这也是一种认识新朋友的方法。他们心里觉得，一个人结婚了，有了一个家，生活才会稳定，才会幸福。这是他们那个年代的想法。
segments.en: But parents see it as one way to meet new people. In their minds, once a person gets married and has a family, life becomes stable and happy. That's how people in their generation think.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 是的，这也是关心。
segments.en: Right, and it comes from caring.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 我懂他们的关心。但是呢，现在的年轻人，包括我自己，想法真的不一样了。以前的人觉得到了年纪就必须结婚。可是现在，很多人觉得不结也没关系。
segments.en: I understand that they care. But young people now, me included, really think differently. People used to feel that once you reached a certain age, you had to get married. But now, a lot of people feel it's fine not to.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 没错。
segments.en: Exactly.

blocks.purpose: 分析不想结婚的第一个现实原因：时间与经济压力

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 那你觉得，为什么现在的年轻人不想那么早结婚，甚至不想相亲呢？
segments.en: Then why do you think young people today don't want to get married that early, or don't even want to go on blind dates?

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 我觉得最重要的一个原因，就是现在的年轻人太忙了，压力也很大。你想想看，很多年轻人在大城市工作，每天早上九点上班，晚上可能要加班到八九点才能回家。周末的时候，只想躺在床上好好休息，或者看看手机、打打游戏。哪里还有时间和精力去认识新的人，去谈恋爱呢？
segments.en: I think the biggest reason is that young people today are just too busy, and the pressure is huge. Think about it: a lot of young people work in big cities, start at nine in the morning, and may have to work overtime until eight or nine at night before they get home. On weekends, they just want to lie in bed and rest, or scroll on their phones and play games. Where is the time or energy to meet new people and date?

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 确实是这样。
segments.en: That's true.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 而且谈恋爱也是需要花时间和金钱的。现在大城市的生活成本很高，租房子、吃饭、坐车，每个月都要花很多钱。很多人觉得，自己一个人的生活都已经很辛苦了。
segments.en: And dating takes time and money too. The cost of living in big cities is high now. Rent, food, transportation — all of it costs a lot every month. A lot of people feel that even living on their own is already exhausting enough.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 然后呢？
segments.en: And then?

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 如果要结婚，还要买房子，还要生孩子、养孩子，那个压力简直不敢想象。所以我身边很多朋友都说，我连自己都养不起，怎么去养一个家庭呢？
segments.en: If you get married, then there's buying a home, having kids, raising kids — that kind of pressure is almost too much to imagine. So a lot of my friends say, “I can barely support myself. How am I supposed to support a family?”

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 太真实了。现在的经济压力和工作节奏，确实让很多人对婚姻感到害怕。没有钱，没有时间，连睡觉的时间都不够，更别说去经营一段感情了。
segments.en: That's so real. The economic pressure and work pace today really do make a lot of people afraid of marriage. No money, no time — sometimes not even enough time to sleep, let alone maintain a relationship.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 是啊。
segments.en: Exactly.

blocks.purpose: 分析不想结婚的第二个原因：向往自由，婚姻变为选修课

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 不过，除了压力大，我觉得还有一个很重要的原因。那就是现在的年轻人，更加重视自己的内心感受和个人自由了。
segments.en: But besides the pressure, I think there's another very important reason. Young people today care much more about their inner feelings and personal freedom.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 怎么说？
segments.en: What do you mean?

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 以前的社会，大家的生活方式很单一，如果不结婚，别人会觉得你很奇怪。但是现在不一样了，现在的社会更加包容。一个人可以生活得非常丰富多彩。你可以去旅行，可以学习新的爱好，可以和朋友一起去吃好吃的。只要你自己觉得开心，结不结婚其实没有那么重要。
segments.en: In the past, society was much more uniform, and if you didn't get married, people thought you were strange. But now it's different. Society is more accepting. One person alone can still have a rich, colorful life. You can travel, pick up new hobbies, go eat good food with friends. As long as you feel happy, whether you marry or not isn't that important.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 嗯。
segments.en: Mm.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 所以，婚姻不再是人生的“必修课”，而变成了一个“选修课”。
segments.en: So marriage is no longer a required course in life. It's become an elective.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 选修课？这个比喻很好。
segments.en: An elective? That's a great comparison.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 对，必修课就是你在学校里必须上的课，不上就不能毕业。选修课就是你可以自己决定上不上，看你自己的兴趣。现在的年轻人觉得，如果遇到一个真正喜欢的人，两个人在一起比一个人更快乐，那就可以结婚。
segments.en: Right. A required course is something you have to take in school — if you don't, you can't graduate. An elective is something you decide whether to take based on your own interests. Young people today feel that if they meet someone they truly like, and being together makes them happier than being alone, then sure, they can get married.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 对的。
segments.en: Exactly.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 但是如果没有遇到，那一个人过也挺好的，完全没有必要为了结婚而结婚。
segments.en: But if they haven't met that person, then living alone can be pretty great too. There's absolutely no need to marry just for the sake of marrying.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 我非常同意！
segments.en: I totally agree!

blocks.purpose: 通过具体朋友的例子，展示高质量单身生活的状态

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 说到这个，我有一个特别好的朋友，她今年三十多岁了。她工作很好，收入也很高。她自己买了一个小房子，养了一只猫。每天下班后，她会去健身房运动，周末和朋友去看电影、逛街。她跟我说，她现在觉得特别自由，特别满足，根本不想为了结婚去改变自己的生活。
segments.en: Speaking of that, I have a very close friend who's already in her thirties. She has a good job and earns well. She bought herself a small apartment and has a cat. After work she goes to the gym, and on weekends she goes to the movies or shopping with friends. She told me that right now she feels incredibly free and satisfied, and she has no desire to change her life just for marriage.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 哇。
segments.en: Wow.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 如果要她和一个不怎么喜欢的人住在一起，每天还要为了谁洗碗、谁做饭这种小事吵架，她觉得那样太痛苦了。所以她宁愿一个人，也不想随便找个人结婚。
segments.en: If she had to live with someone she didn't even like that much and argue every day over little things like who does the dishes and who cooks, she feels that would be miserable. So she'd rather stay single than casually marry just anyone.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 我完全理解你这位朋友的选择。她知道自己想要什么。
segments.en: I completely understand your friend's choice. She knows exactly what she wants.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 嗯。
segments.en: Mm.

blocks.purpose: 解释成语“搭伙过日子”，对比过去和现在对婚姻要求的不同

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 其实，这也说明现在的年轻人对婚姻的要求变高了。过去很多人结婚，可能是为了找个人一起生活，一起分担经济压力。这在中文里有一个说法，叫“搭伙过日子”。
segments.en: Actually, this also shows that young people today have higher expectations of marriage. In the past, many people got married because they wanted someone to live with and to share financial pressure. In Chinese, there's a phrase for that: dāhuǒ guòrìzi.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 搭伙过日子。
segments.en: Getting through life together.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 对。搭伙，就是大家凑在一起。就是两个人像合租的室友一样住在一起，一起吃饭，一起花钱。只要对方没有太大的缺点，就可以过一辈子。但是现在的年轻人不一样。他们要的不是一个合租的室友，而是一个能够理解自己、支持自己、能够进行精神交流的伴侣。
segments.en: Right. Dāhuǒ means getting together or teaming up. It's like two people living together almost like roommates, eating together, spending money together. As long as the other person didn't have any major flaws, they could stay together for life. But young people today are different. They don't want a roommate to simply share a home with. They want a partner who understands them, supports them, and can connect with them on a deeper level.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 没错。
segments.en: Exactly.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 他们更看重爱情本身，更看重两个人在一起开不开心，有没有共同的语言。如果找不到这样的人，那宁愿一直等下去。这其实是对感情更加负责任的表现，而不是随便。
segments.en: They care more about love itself and about whether they are truly happy together, whether they really speak the same language. If they can't find someone like that, they'd rather keep waiting. That's actually a more responsible attitude toward relationships, not being casual.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 路哥，你总结得太好了。其实我们不是排斥婚姻，我们只是排斥那种没有感情基础的、被逼迫的婚姻。只要遇到了对的人，大家还是愿意结婚的。
segments.en: Lu-ge, that was such a good summary. It's not that we're rejecting marriage. We're rejecting the kind of marriage that's pushed on us and built on no emotional foundation. If we meet the right person, people are still willing to get married.

blocks.purpose: 引出与父母观念差异带来的沟通困境

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 可是，这就有了一个很大的问题。我们自己是这么想的，但是怎么让父母理解呢？父母那一辈人的观念，和我们完全是两个世界。
segments.en: But that leads to a big problem. This is how we see it — but how do we get our parents to understand? Their generation's views are almost from a completely different world.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 这是一个世界难题！我每次尝试跟我妈解释我的想法，告诉她我现在一个人挺好的，不着急。她总是听不进去。她会说，你现在年轻觉得好，等老了怎么办？生病了谁照顾你？
segments.en: That's the ultimate impossible problem! Every time I try to explain my thinking to my mom and tell her that I'm actually doing fine on my own and I'm not in a rush, she just can't hear it. She says, “You feel fine now because you're young. What about when you're old? Who will take care of you when you get sick?”

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 哈哈，这是全天下父母通用的台词。
segments.en: Haha, that's the standard line parents everywhere use.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 是啊！她还会说，别人家的孩子都结婚了，你为什么不结婚？然后我们就会开始吵架，最后大家都不开心。真的很让人头疼。路哥，你有什么好办法吗？面对长辈的这种催促，我们年轻人到底应该怎么做比较好？
segments.en: Exactly! And then she says, “Other people's kids are all married, so why aren't you?” Then we start arguing, and in the end nobody feels good. It's such a headache. Lu-ge, do you have any good ideas? When elders pressure us like this, what should young people actually do?

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 哎呀，这确实很难。我觉得最关键的，还是需要沟通，而且是温和的沟通。千万不要吵架。
segments.en: Yeah, that's really hard. I think the key is still communication — gentle communication. Don't argue.

blocks.purpose: 分享温和应对长辈催婚的方法和心态

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 我们不能一听到父母催婚，就马上生气，或者马上反驳他们。因为他们的出发点是好的，是因为爱我们。我们可以先感谢他们的关心，告诉他们我们知道他们是好意。然后，再慢慢地，用他们能听懂的方式，分享我们现在的生活状态。
segments.en: We can't hear our parents push marriage and immediately get angry or start pushing back. Their starting point is good; they do it because they love us. We can thank them first for caring and let them know we understand their good intentions. Then, little by little, we can share our current life with them in a way they can understand.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 慢慢沟通。
segments.en: Talk to them slowly.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 对。比如你可以告诉妈妈，你现在工作很努力，很开心，身边也有很多好朋友，你把自己照顾得很好，让她放心。当父母看到你一个人也能生活得很幸福的时候，他们的焦虑就会慢慢变少。
segments.en: Right. For example, you can tell your mom that you're working hard, that you're happy, that you have lots of good friends around you, and that you're taking good care of yourself, so she doesn't need to worry. When parents can see that you're living well and happily even on your own, their anxiety will gradually lessen.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 嗯，我觉得有道理。
segments.en: Mm, I think that makes sense.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 另外，也不要把话说得太满。不要说“我永远都不结婚”，这样会吓到他们。你可以说，“我还在等那个对的人，如果有遇到合适的，我一定会带回来给你们看”。这样说，父母听了心里也会舒服很多。
segments.en: Also, don't speak too absolutely. Don't say, “I'm never getting married.” That will scare them. You can say, “I'm still waiting for the right person. If I meet someone suitable, I'll definitely bring them home for you to meet.” If you put it that way, your parents will feel much better.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 这个方法好，我下次可以试一试。其实我觉得，两代人之间的想法不同是很正常的。时代的背景变了，社会环境变了，我们的选择当然也会变。我们不需要去改变父母的想法，因为那太难了。
segments.en: That's a good idea. I can try that next time. Honestly, I think it's very normal for two generations to think differently. The times are different, the social environment is different, so of course our choices are different too. We don't need to change our parents' minds, because that's just too hard.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 完全同意。
segments.en: Completely agree.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 我们只需要让他们看到，无论结不结婚，我们都有能力让自己过得好，这就足够了。当他们觉得你长大了，可以对自己负责了，可能就不会那么着急了。
segments.en: We just need to let them see that, whether we get married or not, we have the ability to build a good life for ourselves. That's enough. Once they feel that you've grown up and can take responsibility for yourself, they may stop feeling so anxious.

blocks.purpose: 对今天的讨论进行收束，并引导听众留言互动和订阅

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 是的。婚姻只是一种生活方式的选择，它不是人生的终点，也不是唯一的正确答案。最重要的，是找到让自己觉得最舒服、最快乐的生活状态。
segments.en: Exactly. Marriage is only one way of living. It's not the finish line of life, and it's not the only right answer. What matters most is finding the kind of life that feels most comfortable and happiest for you.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 对。
segments.en: Right.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 如果你现在单身，享受一个人的自由，那很好。如果你遇到了喜欢的人，选择进入婚姻，那也很好。千万不要因为别人的眼光或者父母的催促，去做一个让自己后悔的决定。
segments.en: If you're single right now and enjoying the freedom of being on your own, that's great. If you've met someone you love and choose to get married, that's great too. Just don't make a decision you'll regret because of other people's opinions or your parents' pressure.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 没错。只要认真过好自己的每一天，那就是最好的生活。人生是我们自己的，不是别人的。
segments.en: Exactly. As long as you seriously live your own life well every day, that's the best kind of life. This life is ours, not anyone else's.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 好了，今天我们聊了聊现代年轻人的婚恋观，分享了一些我们自己的看法。听完这期节目，你有什么想法呢？
segments.en: Alright, today we talked about how modern young people view love and marriage, and shared some of our own thoughts. What do you think after listening to this episode?

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 你的父母也会催婚吗？
segments.en: Do your parents pressure you to get married too?

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 你是怎么应对的呢？
segments.en: How do you deal with it?

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 欢迎在评论区留言告诉我们，和大家一起分享你的故事。我们很想听到你们的声音。
segments.en: Leave us a comment and share your story with everyone. We'd really love to hear from you.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 如果你喜欢我们的节目，觉得对你的中文学习有帮助，请记得给我们点赞、订阅我们的频道。
segments.en: If you enjoy our show and feel it helps with your Chinese learning, please remember to like this video and subscribe to our channel.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 也可以分享给你的朋友们！我们下期节目再见。
segments.en: And you can share it with your friends too! See you in the next episode.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 下期再见，拜拜！
segments.en: See you next time. Bye!
